restaurants.name: Floral Cafe at Napasorn

restaurants.ambience: Vibrant indoor floating market–style food hall with traditional boats, wooden décor, and regional Thai music

attractions.name: Safari World Bangkok
attractions.description: Thailand's premier open-zoo and marine entertainment park, divided into Safari Park (drive-through experience with Bengal tigers, giraffes, zebras, rhinos) and Marine Park (dolphin shows, spy war stunt shows, bird shows).
source_files: Thailand/All Inclusive Guide for Bangkok and Pattaya.pdf

attractions.name: Wat Pho
attractions.description: The Temple of the Reclining Buddha
source_files: Thailand/All Inclusive Guide for Pattaya and Bangkok.pdf

attractions.name: Wat Arun
attractions.description: Temple of Dawn
source_files: Thailand/All Inclusive Guide for Pattaya and Bangkok.pdf

attractions.name: Gems Gallery Bangkok
attractions.description: Elegant showroom showcasing Thailand's world-renowned rubies, sapphires, and custom-crafted gemstone jewellery with guided explanation of Thai gem craftsmanship.

attractions.name: Chao Phraya Princess River Cruise
attractions.description: Evening cruise along the Chao Phraya River, passing glittering landmarks like the Grand Palace, Wat Arun, and luxury riverside hotels. Features soft live music, open-air decks, and city reflections in the water.

attractions.name: Asiatique The Riverfront
attractions.description: Riverside area with twinkling lights, boutiques, and Ferris wheel

attractions.name: SEA LIFE Bangkok Ocean World
attractions.description: Thailand's largest aquarium with sharks, penguins, and colorful corals

attractions.name: Grand Palace
attractions.description: Bangkok's most iconic royal complex showcasing traditional Thai architecture and sacred sites. Historic royal complex and Thailand's spiritual-heart royal landmark

attractions.name: Grand Palace & Wat Phra Kaew
attractions.description: Thailand's most sacred royal complex and spiritual landmark — combining exquisite architecture, religious reverence, and the historic heart of Bangkok
attractions.hours: 8:30 AM to 4:30 PM daily; ticket sales end at 3:30 PM
attractions.entry_fee: 500 Baht per person (covers access to the Grand Palace, Wat Phra Kaew, and Queen Sirikit Museum of Textiles); children under 120 cm enter free

attractions.name: Wat Pho (Reclining Buddha)
attractions.description: Wat Pho in Bangkok is famous for its magnificent 46-meter-long Reclining Buddha covered in gold leaf. It's also Thailand's oldest temple complex and a center for traditional Thai massage and healing

attractions.name: ICONSIAM
attractions.description: Bangkok's premier riverside mega mall and cultural hub, blending luxury retail, entertainment, Thai artisan markets, and riverside ambiance on the banks of the Chao Phraya
attractions.hours: Daily from 10:00 AM to 10:00 PM (shops, restaurants)

attractions.name: Safari World & Marine Park
attractions.description: Major wildlife & entertainment complex in Bangkok that combines a drive-through open safari experience and marine animal shows to offer a full animal adventure

attractions.name: KidZania Bangkok
attractions.description: Interactive 'edutainment' indoor city where children engage in role-play of real-world professions (doctor, firefighter, chef, etc.) to learn about responsibility, commerce, teamwork, and societal roles
attractions.hours: Monday – Friday(10:00 AM-5:00 PM) & Saturday, Sunday & Public Holidays(10:30 AM-8:30 PM)
attractions.entry_fee: Children(ages 4–14)-THB 1,100 to THB 1,350, Adults (15+ yrs above)-THB 640 to THB 800, Toddlers (ages 2–3)- THB 700 to THB 875

attractions.name: Or Tor Kor Market
attractions.description: Bangkok's premier 'gourmet' fresh food market, known for its high-quality produce, tropical fruits (especially durian & mango), and clean, well-organized layout — ranked by CNN among the world's top fresh markets

attractions.name: Lumphini Park
attractions.description: The iconic central park in Bangkok, offering lush greenery, a large lake, jogging tracks, public exercise spaces, and a green oasis amid the city's bustle

attractions.name: Science Centre for Education (Bangkok Planetarium)
attractions.description: Major educational complex combining science exhibits, a planetarium dome, natural history displays, an aquarium, and interactive learning — aimed at inspiring curiosity in science and space
attractions.hours: Tuesday to Sunday 9:00 AM to 4:30 PM(closed Mondays & public holidays)
attractions.entry_fee: Adult Price-30 THB, Child / Student Price-20 THB

attractions.name: Art in Paradise 3D Museum
attractions.description: Fun and interactive 3D art museum featuring over 150 optical illusion artworks across six themed zones, offering visitors a unique opportunity to step into and become part of the art
attractions.entry_fee: Adults (foreign visitors): THB 400, Children (height 100–130 cm): THB 200

attractions.name: Terminal 21 Mall
attractions.description: Creatively themed shopping mall where each floor represents a different international city, offering a diverse shopping and dining experience

attractions.name: Ayutthaya Historical Park
attractions.description: UNESCO World Heritage site preserving the ruins of the ancient capital of the Kingdom of Siam (founded in 1351), showcasing historic temples, palaces, Buddha statues, and reflecting Thailand's rich cultural and architectural legacy
attractions.hours: Opens daily at 8:00 AM to 6:00 PM

attractions.name: Museum of Siam
attractions.description: Engaging 'learning museum' that explores Thai identity, history, culture, and traditions through interactive, multimedia exhibits — a fun way for both kids and adults to understand 'Thainess'
attractions.hours: Tuesday – Sunday(10:00 AM – 6:00 PM) Monday Closed

attractions.name: JODD FAIRS night market
attractions.description: Bustling, modern night market combining food, fashion, entertainment, and local culture — a reincarnation of the famed Ratchada Train Night Market with a more convenient city-center location
attractions.hours: Open daily from about 4:00 PM to 12:00 AM (midnight)

attractions.name: Chao Phraya River Cruise
attractions.description: The Chao Phraya River, known as the "River of Kings," flows through Bangkok and is the city's lifeline, lined with historic temples, markets, and modern skyscrapers. A river cruise offers a scenic way to experience Thailand's culture and charm
attractions.entry_fee: THB 134.90 for day pass, around ₹1,927 or USD 30.35 for dinner cruises

attractions.name: Grand Palace & Wat Phra Kaew (Temple of the Emerald Buddha)
attractions.description: The Grand Palace in Bangkok is a dazzling former royal residence, renowned for its ornate architecture and rich history. Within its grounds lies Wat Phra Kaew, home to the revered Emerald Buddha, Thailand's most sacred religious icon

local_dishes.name: Pad Thai
local_dishes.description: Thailand's most famous stir-fried noodle dish with shrimp or chicken, bean sprouts, peanuts & tamarind sauce. Try 'Pad Thai Kung' (with shrimp) for the classic flavor.

local_dishes.name: Green Curry
local_dishes.description: Spicy coconut milk curry with chicken or beef, eggplants, and basil — iconic Thai comfort food. Pair with jasmine rice for balance.

local_dishes.name: Moo Ping
local_dishes.description: Charcoal-grilled pork skewers — one of Thailand's best street snacks. Eat with sticky rice ('Khao Niew') for the true local combo.
local_dishes.where_to_try: Chatuchak Weekend Market or Soi Rambuttri

local_dishes.name: Tom Yum Goong
local_dishes.description: Thailand's signature hot and sour soup made with shrimp, lemongrass, lime, and chili paste. The creamy version (with evaporated milk) is richer and more flavorful.
local_dishes.where_to_try: Pe Aor Tom Yum Kung, Bangkok

local_dishes.name: Khao Man Gai
local_dishes.description: Thai-style Hainanese chicken rice with aromatic rice, boiled chicken, and spicy ginger sauce. Great for breakfast or a light lunch.

local_dishes.name: Som Tam with Grilled Chicken
local_dishes.description: Spicy papaya salad paired with juicy grilled chicken — a signature Isaan (northeast Thai) combo. Ask for medium spice — Thai 'spicy' can be intense!

local_dishes.name: Massaman Curry
local_dishes.description: Mild, rich curry with beef or chicken, potatoes, and peanuts — Persian-influenced southern Thai dish. Perfect for those who prefer less spicy curries.
local_dishes.where_to_try: Blue Elephant Restaurant, Bangkok or Phuket

local_dishes.name: Pla Pao
local_dishes.description: Whole fish stuffed with lemongrass, coated in salt, and grilled over charcoal. Best enjoyed with spicy seafood dipping sauce.

local_dishes.name: Khao Pad
local_dishes.description: Classic Thai fried rice with chicken, shrimp, or crab — simple yet flavorful. Add chili-fish sauce ('Nam Pla Prik') for extra punch.
local_dishes.where_to_try: Any street stall in Bangkok or Phuket

local_dishes.name: Panang Curry
local_dishes.description: Creamy red curry with chicken or beef, thickened with coconut milk and peanuts. Sweeter and milder than red curry — perfect for curry beginners.

local_dishes.name: Pad Thai Jay
local_dishes.description: Stir-fried rice noodles with tofu, peanuts, bean sprouts, and tamarind sauce (vegetarian version).

local_dishes.name: Green Curry with Tofu (Gaeng Keow Wan Jay)
local_dishes.description: Spicy Thai green curry with vegetables and tofu.

local_dishes.name: Red Curry with Vegetables (Gaeng Daeng Jay)
local_dishes.description: Creamy coconut curry with seasonal vegetables and tofu.

local_dishes.name: Massaman Curry with Tofu (Gaeng Massaman Jay)
local_dishes.description: Mild, slightly sweet curry with potatoes, peanuts, and tofu.

local_dishes.name: Tom Yum Soup (Vegetarian Version)
local_dishes.description: Hot and sour soup with mushrooms, lemongrass, lime, and tofu.

local_dishes.name: Som Tam Jay
local_dishes.description: Green papaya salad, often served without fish sauce for the vegetarian version.

local_dishes.name: Spring Rolls (Poh Pia Jay)
local_dishes.description: Crispy vegetable-filled spring rolls.

local_dishes.name: Stir-Fried Morning Glory (Pad Pak Boong Jay)
local_dishes.description: Spicy stir-fried water spinach with garlic and soy sauce.

local_dishes.name: Mango Sticky Rice (Khao Niew Mamuang)
local_dishes.description: Sweet dessert of sticky rice with coconut milk and fresh mango.

local_dishes.name: Fried Tofu with Peanut Sauce
local_dishes.description: Crispy tofu served with a rich peanut dipping sauce.

local_dishes.name: Vegetable Fried Rice (Khao Pad Jay)
local_dishes.description: Thai-style fried rice with assorted vegetables and tofu.

local_dishes.name: Thai Sweet Corn Cakes (Tod Man Khao Pod Jay)
local_dishes.description: Crispy corn fritters, usually vegetarian-friendly.

local_dishes.name: Roti Gluay or fried banana pancakes
local_dishes.description: The warm, crispy pancakes filled with slices of ripe banana, drizzled with condensed milk and chocolate sauce are a sweet treat that you'll love
source_files: Thailand/All Inclusive Guide for Pattaya and Bangkok.pdf

local_dishes.name: Roti Gluay (Fried Banana Pancakes)
local_dishes.description: Warm, crispy pancakes filled with banana slices, drizzled with condensed milk and chocolate sauce
source_files: Thailand/All Inclusive Guide for Bangkok and Pattaya.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Respect the monarchy – Always show respect; avoid negative remarks about the royal family.
Dress appropriately – Cover shoulders and knees when visiting temples.
Use polite gestures – Wai (pressing palms together) is a common greeting.
Bargain politely – Bargaining is common in markets; keep it friendly.
Keep your shoes off – Remove shoes before entering temples and some homes.
Don't touch someone's head – The head is considered sacred in Thai culture.
Don't point your feet – Feet are considered the lowest part of the body; avoid pointing them at people or religious objects.
Don't raise your voice – Loud arguments or confrontations are frowned upon.
Don't litter – Bangkok fines littering; use bins wherever possible.
Don't disrespect monks – Women should not touch monks.
Don't engage in illegal drugs – Strict penalties, including imprisonment.
Stay aware of your surroundings – Pickpocketing can happen in crowded areas and markets
Use reputable transport – Prefer metered taxis, Grab app, BTS/MRT, or licensed tuk-tuks
Keep valuables secure – Use a money belt or hidden pouch for cash, passport, and cards
Avoid unlicensed tours – Book excursions through trusted operators or your hotel
Be cautious with street food – Eat from busy stalls with high turnover for freshness
Avoid scams – Watch out for tuk-tuk scams, gem scams, and overly 'helpful' strangers
Stay hydrated and protected from the sun – Carry water, wear sunscreen and a hat
Follow local laws – Drug offenses and disrespecting the monarchy carry severe penalties
Keep emergency contacts handy – Local police, embassy, and hospital numbers
Avoid isolated areas at night – Stick to well-lit and populated streets
Respect the monarchy – Always show respect; avoid negative remarks about the royal family
Dress appropriately – Cover shoulders and knees when visiting temples
Use polite gestures – Wai (pressing palms together) is a common greeting
Don't touch someone's head – The head is considered sacred in Thai culture
Don't point your feet – Feet are considered the lowest part of the body; avoid pointing them at people or religious objects
Don't raise your voice – Loud arguments or confrontations are frowned upon
Don't disrespect monks – Women should not touch monks

connectivity_tips.tip: AIS (Advanced Info Service) – Widely used, good coverage across the city and tourist areas
DTAC (Total Access Communication) – Strong urban coverage, competitive tourist SIM packages
TrueMove H – Fast 4G/5G network, popular among travelers and locals
Buy SIM cards at Airports (Suvarnabhumi & Don Mueang), 7-Eleven, FamilyMart, mobile network stores (AIS, DTAC, TrueMove H)
Passport required for SIM card registration
Prepaid tourist SIMs available with data, call, and text packages
Top-ups / Reloads available at convenience stores, network stores, or online

transport_options.description: Sky train and metro system covering major tourist areas and shopping districts
Ride-sharing app popular with tourists for reliable transport
Licensed taxis with meters for fair pricing
Traditional three-wheeled vehicles, negotiate fare before riding
Scenic way to experience Thailand's culture and charm along the river
transport_options.recommended_pass: Chao Phraya Tourist Boat day pass

health_tips.tip: Ensure you are up to date on MMR, DTP, polio, and influenza vaccinations
Hepatitis A recommended for most travelers
Hepatitis B recommended if you might have medical procedures or close contact with locals
Typhoid recommended, especially if eating street food
Consider rabies vaccination if you'll have contact with animals or rural travel
Japanese Encephalitis for long-term travelers or those visiting rural areas
Drink bottled or filtered water; avoid tap water
Eat cooked food and fruits you can peel; avoid raw salads from street vendors
Use insect repellent to prevent mosquito-borne diseases (dengue, chikungunya, Zika)
Stay hydrated and protect yourself from heat and sun exposure
Carry a basic first-aid kit and personal medications
Purchase travel insurance covering medical emergencies

source_files: Thailand/All Inclusive Guide for Pattaya and Bangkok.pdf
Thailand/All Inclusive Guide for Bangkok and Pattaya.pdf
